title: Proclamation No. 424, s. 1965
text:
MALACAÑANG

RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 424

AMENDING PROCLAMATION NO. 59 DATED MAY 9, 1936, ESTABLISHING THE MT. APO NATIONAL PARK BY EXCLUDING FROM ITS OPERATION CERTAIN PORTION OF THE NATIONAL PARK FOR AGRICULTURAL PURPOSES.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources, I, Diosdado Macapagal, President of the Philippines, do hereby amend Proclamation No. 59 dated May 9, 1936, which established the Mt. Apo National Park, by excluding from its operation a certain portion of the land embraced therein situated in the Municipality of Kidapawan, Province of Cotabato, and declare the same open to disposition under the Provisions of the Public Land Act, which portion of land is more particularly described as follows:

Sa-V-1608-D (Meliton E. Tupas)

A parcel of land (as shown on plan Sa-V-1608-D, L.R.C. Record No. ‑‑‑‑‑), situated in the Barrio of Miuhaw, Municipality of Kidapawan, Province of Cotabato. Bounded on the N., along lines 1-2-3, by property of Balgos Eagaho; on the E., along line 3-4, by property of Socorro Ichon; on the S., E. and N., along lines 4-5-6-7., by property of Olana Bugaho; on the E., along line 7-8, by property of Joaquin Mostrales; and along line 8-9, by property of Socorro lehon; on the S., and E., along lines 9-10-11, by property of Marcelina Duyan; on the E., along lines 11-12-13, by Apelarino Adang; on the S. and W., along lines from corner 33 thence following the meandering boundary to corner 47, by Small Mateo Creek; on the NE., along line 47-48, by property of Mapza Cayog, along line 48-49, by property of Damo Awan and along line 49-50, by property of Pandis Oean; on the N. amd W., along lines 50-51-52, by property of Kantal Iboy; on the N., along line 52-53, by property of Gregorio Pacate; and on the E., N., and W., along lines 53-54-55-1, by property of Iranon Landim. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 79 deg. 29′ E., 9,665.60 meters from B.L.L.M. 1, Mp. of Kidapawan, Cotabato, thence S. 83 deg. 03′ E., 244.44 meters to point 2; thence S. 72 deg. 55′ E., 38.15 meters to point 3; thence S. 16 deg. 03′ W., 165.24 meters to point 4; thence N. 89 deg. 41′ W., 244.64 meters to point 5; thence S. 2 deg. 33′ W., 197.36 meters to point 6; thence S. 89 deg. 20′ E., 244.44 meters to point 7; thence S. 4 deg 47′ W., 199.30 meters to point 8; thence S. 2 deg. 54′ W., 203.42 meters to point 9; thence N. 88 deg. 25′ W., 248.01 meters to point 10; thence S. 2 deg. 23′ W., 196.34 meters to point 11; thence S. 3 deg. 02′ W., 203.62 meters to point 12; thence S. 5 deg. 20′ W,. 37.86 meters to point 13; thence N. 79 deg. 23′ W., 35.18 meters to point 14; thence N. 34 deg. 58′ W., 27.50 meters to point 15; thence S. 67 deg. 25′ W., 23.07 meters to point 16; thence N. 88 deg. 59′ W., 117.60 meters to point 17; thence S. 62 deg. 40′ W., 36.17 meters to point 18; thence N. 79 deg. 46′ W., 13.17 meters to point 19; thence N. 53 deg. 57′ W., 58.59 meters to point 20; thence S., 57 deg. 19′ W., 24.59 meters to point 21; thence N. 43 deg. 19′ W., 22.42 meters to point 22; thence S. 64 deg. 00′ W., 57.62 meters to point 23; thence N. 59 deg. 09′ W., 75.83 meters to point 24; thence S. 68 deg. 55′ W,, 20.29 meters to point 25; thence S. 89 deg. 50′ W., 32.35 meters to point 26; thence N. 89 deg. 07′ W., 50.42 meters to point 27; thence N. 69 deg. 06′ W., 19.51 meters to point 28; thence N. 77 deg. 58′ W., 68.00 meters to point 29; thence N. 79 deg. 27′ W., 58.95 meters to point 30; thence N. 72 deg. 38′ W., 64.60 meters to point 31; thence N. 78 deg. 59′ W., 66.93 meters to point 32; thence N. 58 deg. 54′ W., 157.32 meters to point 33; thence N. 71 deg. 45′ W., 161.42 meters to point 34; thence S. 77 deg. 44′ W., 23.85 meters to point 35; thence N. 70 deg. 55′ W., 33.41 meters to point 36; thence N. 67 deg. 19′ W., 64.96 meters to point 37; thence N. 43 deg. 22′ W., 30.14 meters to point 38; thence S. 86 deg. 54′ W., 41.12 meters to point 39; thence S. 64 deg. 48′ W., 38.05 meters to point 40; thence N. 12 deg. 18′ E., 46.69 meters to point 41; thence N. 46 deg. 00′ W., 30.97 meters to point 42; thence S. 83 deg. 59′ W., 98.96 meters to point 43; thence S. 11 deg. 33′ E., 39.80 meters to point 44; thence N. 81 deg. 12′ W., 60.94 meters to point 45; thence N. 1 deg. 19′ E., 44.04 meters to point 46; thence N. 19 deg. 26′ W., 202.07 meters to point 47; thence N. 69 deg. 36′ E., 443.23 meters to point 48; thence N. 44 deg. 59′ E., 456.72 meters to point 49; thence N. 40 deg. 15′ E., 89.85 meters to point 50; thence S. 89 deg. 33′ E., 289.64 meters to point 51; thence N. 5 deg. 59′ W., 229.68 meters to point 52; thence S. 86 deg. 25′ E., 298.15 meters to point 53; thence S. 5 deg. 05′ E., 198.85 meters to point 54; thence S. 75 deg. 39′ E., 227.04 meters to point 55; thence N. 3 deg. 03′ E., 188.56 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 1,404,105 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and marked on the ground as follows: points 14 to 46 (inclusive) by Stakes and the rest by P.L.S. Cyl. Conc. Mors.; bearings true; date of survey, Sept. 30-Oct. 2, 1950 approved on Dec. 29, 1950.

NOTE: This is Lot 4220 of Kidapawan Public Land Subdivision Pls-59 and claimed by the Bureau of Forestry to be inside the Apo National Park Reservation.

IN WITNESS .WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 28th day of July, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-five.

DIOSDADO MACAPAGAL President of the Philippines

By the President:

JUAN S. CANCIO Acting Assistant Executive Secretary

Source: University of the Philippines, College of Law Library
